column21: My campaign is on "Ending Stigma and Discrimination Against LBQ Women". LBQ Women have for the longest time suffered stigma and discrimination due to our sexual orientation which has led to a high rise in mental health conditions amongst our minority group. Lack of proper health care has sadly led to a rise in HIV/STI infections with many of us self medicating for fear of accessing medical facilities. It's a fundamental human right to have better health care and no woman should be denied access to medical services just because she identifies  differently. My goal is to 1.Have more empowered and sensitized health care providers in the rural areas, who will attend to LBQ women without subjecting them to more humiliation. 2.Raise more awareness on the issues affecting LBQ women in the rural areas e.g Inequality, lack of better health care and education which are all root causes of stigma and discrimination. 3. As an organization we are also looking at having a 'Queer Clinic' with the support of one of our allies Marie Stopes International where LBQ Women can access medical services at any time under the watch of qualified medical personnel. We demand for an equal and inclusive health care system.
